epitaphs: He was sure his eyes were {claimed}. The pond, unbothered, remains {truth}.
"{claimed}," he swore to the guard — and the wall wrote {truth} on his stone.
He gambled on {claimed}. The ledger of his eyes had always read {truth}.
The guard heard {claimed}; the truth, patient as the gate itself, was {truth}.
The woods gave three warnings. He read none of them, and the fourth had teeth.
He lingered on the path until the path grew a mouth. It closed patiently.
The howls were for him all along. He learned this at the very end, briefly.
He mistook the growl for scenery. The wolf did not mistake him for anything.
